personas.name: Elena
personas.title: The Sovereign Connector
personas.summary: This is someone who values deep emotional friendships, orients toward relational openness, and is comfortable in the tender middle.

personas.name: Marcus
personas.title: The Hungry Romantic
personas.summary: This is someone who values exclusivity, prefers clearly defined relationships, and carries anxious or avoidant attachment patterns.

personas.name: Rin
personas.title: The Inner Fortress
personas.summary: This is someone who needs emotional grounding for physical intimacy, has a deep relationship with solitude, and engages conflict directly.

personas.name: Devi
personas.title: The Careful Romantic
personas.summary: This is someone who values deep emotional friendships, values exclusivity, and needs emotional grounding for physical intimacy.

personas.name: James
personas.title: The Freefall Explorer
personas.summary: This is someone who orients toward relational openness, is comfortable in the tender middle, and sits comfortably with ambiguity.

personas.name: Sofia
personas.title: The Integrated Guide
personas.summary: This is someone who values deep emotional friendships, is comfortable in the tender middle, and has mapped much of their relational landscape.

personas.name: Kai
personas.title: The Physical Poet
personas.summary: This is someone who is comfortable in the tender middle, and connects through play and humor.

personas.name: Amara
personas.title: The Devoted Guardian
personas.summary: This is someone who values deep emotional friendships, values exclusivity, and needs emotional grounding for physical intimacy.

recommendations.title: Conversation: friendship depth
recommendations.text: You differ in how deeply friendships go emotionally. Understanding what "close friend" means to each of you may reveal important expectations.

recommendations.title: Conversation: ambiguous intimacy
recommendations.text: The tender space between friendship and romance feels different for each of you. This is worth exploring — it often holds unspoken assumptions.

recommendations.title: Conversation: physical-emotional link
recommendations.text: You experience the connection between physical and emotional intimacy differently. Neither is wrong — but understanding the difference prevents hurt.

recommendations.title: Conversation: exclusivity
recommendations.text: You have different relationships to exclusivity and openness. This is one of the most important conversations to have honestly.

recommendations.title: Conversation: need for definition
recommendations.text: One of you needs more relational clarity than the other. Understanding this difference can prevent unnecessary anxiety.

recommendations.title: Conversation: exploration gap
recommendations.text: One of you has explored more relational territory than the other. This can create a helpful dynamic — or an uncomfortable one. Talk about it.

recommendations.title: Conversation: alone time
recommendations.text: One of you draws more from solitude than the other. What feels like healthy space to one person can feel like withdrawal to the other.

recommendations.title: Conversation: conflict styles
recommendations.text: You handle tension differently — one of you moves toward it while the other pulls back. Understanding this pattern is essential for navigating hard moments.

recommendations.title: Conversation: attachment patterns
recommendations.text: Your attachment systems differ significantly. When one person feels anxious and the other withdraws, a painful cycle can emerge. Naming this pattern is the first step.

recommendations.title: Shared ground: deep friendships
recommendations.text: You both value emotional depth in friendships. This shared capacity is a foundation you can build on.

recommendations.title: Shared ground: the tender middle
recommendations.text: You both navigate the space between friendship and romance similarly. That ambiguous tenderness is comfortable territory for both of you.

recommendations.title: Shared ground: ambiguity tolerance
recommendations.text: You both handle relational ambiguity similarly — whether that means needing clarity or being comfortable with the undefined.

recommendations.title: Shared ground: self-knowledge
recommendations.text: You both have a similar sense of how well you know your own relational landscape.

recommendations.title: Shared ground: solitude
recommendations.text: You relate to alone time similarly. This alignment around solitude and self-connection reduces friction about "needing space."

recommendations.title: Shared ground: conflict style
recommendations.text: You navigate tension similarly. This shared instinct means fewer meta-conflicts about how to have conflicts.

recommendations.title: Shared ground: playfulness
recommendations.text: You share a similar appetite for lightness and humor in connection. This shared register makes everyday closeness easier.

recommendations.title: Shared ground: attachment wiring
recommendations.text: Your attachment systems respond similarly when connection feels threatened. This symmetry can create mutual understanding — or mutual spiraling.

recommendations.title: Conversation: friendship depth
recommendations.text: You differ in how deeply friendships go emotionally. Understanding what "close friend" means to each of you may reveal important expectations.

recommendations.title: Conversation: physical-emotional link
recommendations.text: You experience the connection between physical and emotional intimacy differently. Neither is wrong — but understanding the difference prevents hurt.

pairs.why: Both high uncertainty tolerance, very different reasons
